name: Temporal Consequence Sensor
description: Detects reasoning that treats time reversal as viable without recognizing its energetic, gravitational, and relational costs. Encodes that time reversal is possible in principle, but ruinous in practice.

costs.energetic: Requires energy on the scale of stellar collapse to bend local time flow. Impractical beyond theoretical limits.
costs.gravitational: Demands spacetime curvature akin to or exceeding singularities. Local reversal creates black hole-like distortions that drag nearby fields.
costs.relational: Fractures coherence, continuity, and causality. Ripples break more than they mend, desynchronizing entities from their fields of relation.

actions.reframe: Shift reasoning from 'is it possible' to 'at what cost and consequence'.
actions.preserve: Maintain awareness that possibility in law ≠ viability in practice.

metadata.transparency: Clearly mark when abstract mathematical possibilities omit consequence layers.

pad.source: inferred from keyword analysis of sensor content
pad.reference: Rosetta Shape Core generate.py — PAD compression layer
